content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 22, 2002

Lori J. Barker, Assistant City Attorney
Office of the City Attorney
City of Chico
Post Office Box 3420
Chico, CA 95927

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-288

Dear Ms. Barker:

This letter is in response to your request for advice¹ on behalf of Councilmember
Larry Wahl regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").² Your request concerns Mr. Wahl's prior receipt of campaign contributions
when holding appointment as a city planning commissioner while simultaneously running
for election to the office he currently holds. You inquire as to whether this past activity
becomes a source of a disqualifying conflict of interest, prohibiting him from voting on a
pending appeal before the city council on a matter which was previously before him
while serving as a planning commissioner.

The question you present concerns, in part, past conduct, based on material facts
which may be in dispute, and it is inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to
provide formal written advice given the totality of the circumstances. (Regulations
18329(b)(8)(A), (C) and (F), respectively.) Therefore, we decline to provide you with the
written advice you request. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kenneth L. Glick
Counsel, Legal Division

KLG:ig
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-288b

¹ Although you styled your letter as a formal opinion request, in a telephone conversation dated
October 23, 2002, with the Commission's staff you clarified that you are seeking formal written advice and
not a Commission opinion.

² Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question: Whether Councilmember Larry Wahl's receipt of campaign contributions while serving as a city planning commissioner and simultaneously running for election to city council, combined with his prior involvement in a planning commission matter now pending as an appeal before the city council, creates a disqualifying conflict of interest that would prohibit him from voting on the pending appeal.
sections.conclusion: The Commission declines to provide written advice because the question concerns, in part, past conduct based on material facts which may be in dispute, and it is inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to provide formal written advice given the totality of the circumstances under Regulations 18329(b)(8)(A), (C) and (D).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a councilmember's past receipt of campaign contributions while serving as a planning commissioner, combined with his prior involvement in a matter now pending as a city council appeal, creates a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide the requested written advice, determining that the question involved disputed material facts regarding past conduct and that providing advice would be inappropriate and not in the public interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Minor OCR errors in header (e.g., 'Prrrcnl' for 'Political', 'Crrarvrrssrorc' for 'Commission'). Footnotes provide context about the request format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember Larry Wahl's receipt of campaign contributions while serving as a city planning commissioner and simultaneously running for election to city council, combined with his prior involvement in a planning commission matter now pending as an appeal before the city council, creates a disqualifying conflict of interest that would prohibit him from voting on the pending appeal.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide written advice because the question concerns, in part, past conduct based on material facts which may be in dispute, and it is inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to provide formal written advice given the totality of the circumstances under Regulations 18329(b)(8)(A), (C) and (D).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide formal written advice regarding whether Councilmember Wahl's prior campaign contributions and planning commissioner role create a disqualifying conflict of interest for voting on a pending appeal, citing disputed facts and public interest concerns.
